page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with a single thread per core. This instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe SSD storage or hardware GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance. The r8gb.8xlarge achieves top-tier results in uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth, as well as in PassMark memory and database operations tests. Multi-core processing is also highly competitive, with Geekbench multi-core scores ranking in the top tier. Single-core processing and standard integer operations, such as stress-ng CPU tests and PassMark encryption, fall within the average range. For specialized tasks, the instance exhibits strong performance in software compilation, image processing, and text processing, while showing average to poor performance in large-scale LLM text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers a highly favorable resource density for memory-intensive workloads without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. However, the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for high-speed local scratch space requirements or heavy parallel computing tasks. The r8gb.8xlarge is optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput static web serving, and data decompression pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a 67 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier performance in uncached memory bandwidth, PassMark memory scores, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. Conversely, single-core CPU performance and LLM text generation speeds remain in the average range. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads by leveraging the ARM64 architecture. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated ARMv9 cores, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 67 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for demanding database and caching workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier uncached memory bandwidth and database operation performance

Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and data processing
